question.content: Build the following structural model: a closed square rigid frame with side length a has identical flexural rigidity EI on all four sides. A uniform shear flow q of equal magnitude acts tangentially along each member axis and is continuously transferred around the closed boundary. Using left-right structural symmetry and load antisymmetry, cut the frame at the midpoint B of the top side and keep only one vertical cut shear F_SB at the cut. Analyze the right half frame along the path: half of the top side, full right vertical side, and half of the bottom side. Then apply a unit vertical virtual force at B; using virtual work and displacement compatibility at the cut, determine F_SB. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: By symmetry and antisymmetry, after cutting at B the only cut internal force is the vertical shear F_SB. For the force-method equation, apply a unit vertical virtual force at B and use Delta_BB'=0.
step_1.key_point: Set up the single redundant F_SB and the virtual-work compatibility equation at the cut.

step_4.content: On the three path segments of the right half, use coordinates x1, x2, x3 and write the virtual and real bending moments: m1(x1)=x1, M1(x1)=F_SB*x1, 0<=x1<=a/2; m2(x2)=a/2, M2(x2)=F_SB*(a/2)-(a/2)*q*x2, 0<=x2<=a; m3(x3)=a/2-x3, M3(x3)=F_SB*(a/2-x3)-(1/2)*q*a*a-q*a*x3, 0<=x3<=a/2.
step_4.key_point: Write piecewise m and M expressions for top half-span, right side, and bottom half-span.

step_6.content: Virtual-work compatibility: Delta_BB'=(2/(E*I))*(integral_0^(a/2)[m1(x1)M1(x1)dx1]+integral_0^a[m2(x2)M2(x2)dx2]+integral_0^(a/2)[m3(x3)M3(x3)dx3])=0. After integration and simplification, F_SB=5*q*a/8.
step_6.key_point: Substitute the three segment integrals into Delta_BB'=0 and solve for F_SB.

step_8.content: Therefore, the cut shear is F_SB=5*q*a/8.
step_8.key_point: Final value of the cut shear force at B.
